text: This study conducted a systematic literature review (SLR) aimed at analyzing the trends of research on STEM and STEAM education in Indonesia in the period 2016–2021. According to the PRISMA (Preferred Reporting Items for Systematic Reviews and Meta-Analyses) principles and the Paper Classification Form (PCF) format, 63 papers obtained from domestic and foreign publications were analyzed. The results show that the publications in Indonesia on STEM and STEAM are increasing significantly within the last six years, with the majority employing the experimental method. The largest number of papers dealt with learning strategies, particularly focusing on science subjects in secondary school students. It is found that the frameworks employed in project-based learning (PjBL), problem-based learning (PBL), and inquiry-based learning (IBL) for integrating STEM/STEAM in the classroom are the most dominant. The dominant skills acquired by students from STEM/STEAM learning activities are those of critical thinking, scientific literacy, and general learning skills. Therefore, STEM education has been developed maturely in Indonesia. It is urgently needed to conduct more comprehensive and empirical research related to STEAM education in Indonesia in order to analyze and answer the role of the arts and the role of technology in developing 21st-century skills.
